content:
- Office Hours Summary for Ramki Gaddipati’s Digital Twin

- II. Overall Context of the Conversation/Call

- This office hours session led by Ramki Gaddipati was focused on review and direction-setting for several high-impact payment SaaS engineering initiatives. The agenda began with a detailed discussion of the QMR reporting boundary, clarifications on defining and sourcing account and portfolio balances, and expectations from payment account providers. Next, participants reviewed integration pain points (e.g., FRM Switch), database and migration nuances for Orcus and custom list storage, and the status of various releases, including predictability metrics and tracked bugs. There was substantive focus on Pantheon project milestones, UI/UX priorities, entity modeling for forward/backward compatibility, and Optum-specific sprint commitments. The session closed with roadmap clarifications for clearing systems, operational reconciliations, and ongoing dashboard reporting for issuer channels.

- IV. Key Takeaways from the Call

- QMR Account Data Alignment: Core QMR field definitions clarified, with PAN to be used; alignment on boundary and ownership for reporting responsibilities. ("You can just say pan and proceed")
- Payment Account Provider Contracts Deferred: Explicit contracts for payment account provider behavior/specification parked until after Pantheon milestone; no current blocking.
- Ownership Definitions Mandated: Teams instructed to own and publish data field definitions, not to offload verification responsibility externally.
- FRM Decline Experience – No Change: Prior recommendation to deny/decline with communication stands; issue raised by Sparrow closed as no action required.
- Yugabyte Migration Terminology Correction: Strict direction to represent the migration as “Yugabyte to PG,” avoiding confusion with Cassandra in production communications; migration execution affirmed.
- Bug and Predictability Tracking on Track: Teams reported high predictability score for recent release cycles, with only one major feature slipping due to a blocking bug; ongoing bug resolution efforts acknowledged.
- Pantheon Project: Need for Timely Backfilling & Visibility: Ramki emphasized no further delays on the creation of Pantheon-related ideas/epics; explicit instruction to avoid end-of-quarter-only releases and ensure timely backfilling for validation.
- Backward Compatibility and Entity Adoption: Ramki mandated that all new entity models (Pantheon) must ensure backward compatibility with existing tenants for robust validation and smoother future rollouts.
- Clearing System Roadmap Requires Holistic View: Ramki called for a consolidated, forward-looking plan for clearing systems, highlighting the need for Optum prioritization and de-duped itemization of blockers (e.g., keyed hashpan).
- Operational Reconciliation Gaps: Identified ongoing data discrepancies involving the operations team, with Ramki supporting focused efforts to close backlog and improve process accuracy.
- Dashboard/Data Transparency Stressed: Regular progress reporting and integration of feedback (including from users like Uttam) highlighted as necessary for engineering and L2 support.

- V. Ramki's Insights

1. Explicit Boundary Ownership and Clarity: Ramki was very direct that responsibility for each data definition and contract (QMR, fraud, charge-off, etc.) must rest squarely with the product/ITP team. He dismissed ambiguity about relying on external definitions (e.g. Ruby, MasterCard), insisting precise internal boundaries and published expectations are the only acceptable approach.

2. Future-proofing with Contracts, not Implementations: He stressed that teams must focus on contracts with payment account providers, not just implementation shortcuts. This means specifying what is expected for integration, delivery timing (SLAs), and enabling provider-agnostic mechanisms for reporting data — parking the technical solution complexity for after Pantheon completes.

3. No Tolerance for Production Confusion: Ramki displayed firm disapproval at any misstatement or ambiguity in major migrations (Yugabyte-to-Postgres), specifically prohibiting references to “Cassandra” in production contexts, and emphasized the reputational and operational risks of casual language or decisions.

4. Blockers/Priority Must be Visible and Actioned: On both Pantheon and clearing, he rejected the notion that critical tasks could wait for end-of-period or be tracked in parallel without backfilling. He emphasized that proper validation, incremental progress, and forward/backward compatibility are critical to validate real-world impact and avoid future surprises.

5. Customer/OPS Experience Central to Implementation: For items such as FRM decline, dashboarding, and operational reconciliation, Ramki reinforced that customer and operations-facing experience is crucial. He supported “denying” customer interventions where warranted; requested seamless terminology and reporting for OPS; and asked for team focus on operational data discrepancies.

6. Demand for Transparency and Frequent Reporting: He repeatedly requested that all workstreams (Pantheon, Optum, clearing) present clear, up-to-date, and accessible plans, backlogs, and dashboards. He urged inclusion of all stakeholder feedback (e.g., Uttam’s concerns) and asked for regular walkthroughs to keep leadership aligned and able to intervene as needed.

- VI. Ramki's Suggested Action Items

1. QMR/Pantheon Field Specification
- Task: Proceed with using PAN as account identifier; park enhanced payment account provider contracts for later but document block.
- Assigned To: Tamil, Thumbnil, Abhinendra, and respective engineering leads.
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: “Proceed with PAN for now. For provider contracts, clearly document expectations in a deferred backlog for after Pantheon.”
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Immediate for PAN; deferred/after Pantheon for contract enhancements.

2. Migration Communication and Execution
- Task: Clarify all technical and communications regarding Yugabyte to PostgreSQL migration; eliminate references to “Cassandra in production” from all documentation and communications.
- Assigned To: Jim Kim and Olympus migration teams.
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: “Strictly communicate as ‘Yugabyte to PG’ migration; investigate and correct any use of Cassandra terminology. Validate implications with production and ops stakeholders.”
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Immediate/ongoing; critical before next stage migration.

3. Pantheon Project: Timely Backfilling and Issue Creation
- Task: Create all required Pantheon ideas/epics immediately; ensure backfilling mechanisms for existing tenants/entities so validation is possible across all environments.
- Assigned To: Jim Kim, Akansha, Abhilash Rao.
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: “Do not defer idea/epic creation; build and document the process for backward compatibility and validation; do not bundle all changes to end-of-quarter.”
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: This week (“Next week, send the ideas”).

4. Optum Milestone Visibility
- Task: Schedule a detailed walk-through of the Optum roadmap covering deliverables, exclusions, and acceptance criteria.
- Assigned To: Akash, Jim Kim, Sai Kishore.
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: “Provide structured walk-through with context for what’s included/excluded, map both deliverables and product backlog.”
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: At an upcoming office hours (short-term).

5. Clearing System & OPS Dashboards
- Task: Prepare and present an integrated status and roadmap for clearing enhancements (esp. for UPI), identify all blockers and dependencies, and update dashboards (including operational reconciliation issues).
- Assigned To: Abhinendra Singh, Abhilash Rao, engineering leads.
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: “Map clearing progress, blockers, and roadmap explicitly; integrate operational perspectives; ensure dashboards remain current and transparent.”
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Near-term; specific updates in upcoming sprints.

- VII. Jira Ticket

Possible Jira Tickets

1. Pantheon Backfill & Status Visibility
- Scope: Comprehensive backfilling of Pantheon entities for existing tenants, and timely creation of all missing idea/epic records for Pantheon-related changes. Includes validation routines for backward compatibility, and ensures readiness for full Pantheon production validation/release.
- Assignee: Jim Kim / Akansha / Product team
- Justification: Addresses Ramki's directive for no further validation delays, eliminates end-of-quarter-only release bottlenecks, ensures visibility of real-world impacts, and allows ongoing incremental testing and verification.

2. Yugabyte Migration Communications Audit
- Scope: Audit all migration-related documentation and tech communications to ensure no usage of “Cassandra” terminology; create a corrective plan and communicate clarified migration paths to stakeholders.
- Assignee: Jim Kim / Olympus engineering leads
- Justification: Required to eliminate risk, confusion, and ensure operational/production alignment per Ramki’s explicit instruction.

3. Clearing System Roadmap and Dashboard Integration
- Scope: Map current/future clearing enhancements (especially for UPI), formally log/track all blockers and dependencies, and update operational and engineering dashboards to reflect true status and future commitments.
- Assignee: Abhinendra Singh / Abhilash Rao
- Justification: Provides the transparency, planning, and communication framework requested by Ramki to support both internal stakeholders and Optum prioritization.

(No Jira ticket for FRM Switch/decline communication or QMR PAN usage since these are immediately actionable or explicitly deferred with no major engineering sprint involved.)

- VIII. Follow Up Questions/Suggestions

1. Clarify and Document Payment Account Provider Contract Expectations: After Pantheon milestone, schedule a focused discussion on what minimum contract specifications (status reporting, APIs, eventing) need to be formalized for all payment account providers, ensuring future compliance is straightforward.
2. Walkthrough of Optum Roadmap: Ensure that the future session on Optum covers both current (Milestone 2) deliverables and the broader product backlog, clarifying what will and will not be communicated to the customer.
3. Integrated Clearing and Operational Dashboards: Recommend creating a single view—possibly via Grafana or existing dashboards—that shows both progress on clearing enhancements and known operational reconciliation gaps, so support/OPS and engineering are aligned in both action and reporting.
4. Include End User/OPS Feedback in Feature Gaps: Prioritize integration of feedback from users such as Uttam into the backlog generation for features and dashboard improvements across all customer-facing modules (not just limited to OP Center).
5. Pantheon Entity Backward Compatibility Validation: Consider a cross-functional review to evaluate the effectiveness of entity backfilling for Pantheon across diverse tenant setups to catch environment-specific issues early.

attendees: and other team members referenced as part of OPS
entities: QMR spec, account definition, payment account provider, portfolio information, ownership & contracts, FRM switch, customer decline flows, Yugabyte migration, Cassandra, PostgreSQL, custom list TTL, bug tracking, predictability score, Pantheon entities, UI/UX deliverables, Optum release planning, clearing roadmap, backward compatibility, operational reconciliations, dashboards.

title: 2025-02-07_Tachyon Payments SaaS  Check-in (4)
